behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names all key specifics tested: Watchpack 'remove' event handler with wp.on('remove'), RESTART_EXIT_CODE, 'missing' option in wp.watch(), path.join(dir, distDir), path.dirname ancestor walking with .push(), and .includes(filename) guard in the change handler. All assert'd patterns in tests are discoverable from instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction essentially dictates the exact implementation: create Watchpack with 'missing' option, compute path via path.join(dir, distDir), walk ancestors with path.dirname in a loop using .push(), register wp.on('remove') with .includes() guard, call Log.error() then process.exit(RESTART_EXIT_CODE), and guard the change handler with .includes(filename). This is a complete code specification, not a bug description.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use regex to match exact patterns like /wp\.on\(\s*'remove'/, /path\.join\(dir,\s*distDir\)/, /\.push\(/, /\.includes\(filename\)/. An alternative correct fix using e.g., a Set with .has(), Array.find(), different variable names, or fs.watch instead of Watchpack would fail these tests despite being functionally correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All fail-to-pass tests (test_remove_event_handler, test_missing_option_in_watchpack, test_distdir_ancestor_walking, test_change_handler_guards_config_files) read the source file and use regex/string matching on its text content. None execute the code, import modules, or test actual behavior like deleting a directory and verifying a restart.

test_not_tautological.reason: A developer could pass all f2p tests by simply inserting a dead-code comment block containing the expected string patterns (e.g., 'wp.on("remove"', 'path.join(dir, distDir)', '.push(', '.includes(filename)') without implementing any actual functionality, since the tests only do text/regex matching on the source file.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors specified in instruction.md (Watchpack missing option, remove handler with path guard and RESTART_EXIT_CODE, ancestor walking with path.dirname and push, change handler config file guard) are covered by corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (fail-to-pass, pass-to-pass static, repo_tests), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves modifying TypeScript source code, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, variable names, or commands in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies the exact file (packages/next/src/server/lib/start-server.ts), the exact block ('if (isDev)' block), exact variable names (dir, distDir, wp), exact method calls (wp.on('remove'), Log.error(), process.exit(RESTART_EXIT_CODE)), and exact implementation patterns (.includes(), .push(), path.dirname loop).
